channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Oil prices drop as U.S.-Iran peace talks remain uncertain
item.description: Oil prices experienced a decline of more than US$2 on Monday as investors awaited further clarity on the status of U.S.-Iran peace negotiations. Despite a significant surge in prices the previous Thur...

item.title: Police Investigating Connections Between Ryan Wedding-linked Homicide and Unsolved Cases
item.description: Two years after the killing of suspected drug trafficker Randy Fader in Niagara Falls, Ont., allegedly targeted by Ryan Wedding's criminal network, police continue to probe his murder. The shooting, w...

item.title: Honouring the Legacy and Lasting Impact of the TAZARA Railway
item.description: The Tanzania-Zambia Railway (TAZARA), built in the 1970s, remains a powerful symbol of friendship, sacrifice, and cooperation between Tanzania, Zambia, and China. Recently, CRJE (East Africa) Ltd, tog...

item.title: NSX prepares to become Australia’s third stock trading platform
item.description: The National Stock Exchange of Australia (NSX) is gearing up to establish itself as the country’s third stock trading venue, aiming to rival the ASX and Cboe Australia. Acquired last year by CNSX Glob...

item.title: Ontario government remains tight-lipped about the cost of its ‘Protect Ontario’ ad campaign
item.description: The Ontario government is currently running a wide-ranging advertising campaign featuring commercials that end with the phrase 'Protect Ontario,' tied to the Progressive Conservatives' election slogan...

item.title: Pakistan Government and Provinces Announce Measures to Cope with Energy Shortage
item.description: As soaring global energy prices—driven by the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran—intensify Pakistan’s energy crisis, both the federal and provincial governments have introduced emergency measures to manage...

item.title: Koko Networks Enters Administration After Failing to Secure Carbon Credit Approval
item.description: Koko Networks, a Nairobi-based bioethanol fuel and clean cookstove company once valued at around KSh 22 billion, has gone into administration after financial struggles linked to carbon credit approval...

item.title: Families face prolonged delays reclaiming deposits from Scottish care homes
item.description: Numerous families in Scotland have expressed deep frustration after enduring lengthy struggles to recover significant sums of money from care home operator Morar Care Group, with deposits reaching up ...

item.title: BIR sets clearer guidelines for tax incentives in education collaborations
item.description: The Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) announced a new policy to simplify how private companies can qualify for tax breaks when supporting education programs in the Philippines. Through the release of R...

item.title: Magnitude 5.4 Earthquake Hits Off Cebu, Aftershocks Expected
item.description: On April 6, 2026, a magnitude 5.4 earthquake struck off the island province of Cebu, with its epicenter located 17 kilometers east of Bogo City. The tremor occurred at around 3:22 p.m., and its effect...

item.title: Astrological Forecasts for April 5, 2026 by Georgia Nicols
item.description: The Chicago Sun-Times published its daily horoscope for Sunday, April 5, 2026, written by astrologer Georgia Nicols. The article opens with a 'Moon Alert,' advising readers to avoid major decisions an...

item.title: Bonelala Mgudlwa to appeal R14 million PPE contract ruling in High Court
item.description: Businessman Bonelala Mgudlwa has rejected the recent decision by the Special Tribunal, which declared two COVID-19 personal protective equipment (PPE) contracts awarded by the Mpumalanga Department of...

item.title: The Lopez Family Feud and the Future of Their Empire
item.description: The ongoing feud within the Lopez family has taken a dramatic turn, with Federico 'Piki' Lopez being ousted from his position as president and CEO of Lopez Incorporated, a company that has been an inf...

item.title: Shark Bite Scares Teen Surfer at Middleton Beach
item.description: A 16-year-old surfer, Oliver Tokic-Bensley, had a frightening experience at Middleton Beach in South Australia over the Easter weekend when he was bitten on the foot by a shark. The incident occurred ...

item.title: Comprehensive Analysis Confirms Predictable Patterns in Global Language Evolution
item.description: A large-scale study conducted by an international research team from the Max Planck Society and Saarland University has revealed that languages, despite their apparent diversity, evolve in predictable...

item.title: Researchers Identify Brain Cells That Facilitate Glioblastoma Growth and Potential Treatment
item.description: Researchers at McMaster University and The Hospital for Sick Children in Canada have uncovered a surprising mechanism by which glioblastoma, one of the most aggressive and deadly brain cancers, grows....

item.title: How Gaviotas Built a Sustainable Future in Colombia’s Harsh Plains
item.description: In the heart of Colombia’s eastern savannah, a remote community called Gaviotas has become a remarkable model of sustainability and self-reliance. Founded in the early 1970s by Paolo Lugari, Gaviotas ...

item.title: Arvind Kejriwal to Represent Himself in Delhi HC Over Judge Recusal in Excise Policy Case
item.description: Arvind Kejriwal, national convenor of the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP), is set to personally argue his recusal application before the Delhi High Court regarding Justice Swarn Kanta Sharma in the alleged exci...

item.title: India’s Glass Industry Faces Severe Gas Shortages Amid Gulf War Disruptions
item.description: Firozabad, known as India’s ‘Glass City’ for over four centuries, is facing a serious crisis as the Gulf war has disrupted its crucial gas supplies, forcing furnaces to operate below normal levels. Th...

item.title: The Last Resident of Lesotho’s Kome Caves and the Community’s Efforts to Preserve Their Heritage
item.description: In Lesotho’s Berea district, approximately 25 kilometres east of Teyateyaneng, the historic Kome Caves stand as a living monument to the nation’s past. Once a refuge for families escaping the wars and...
